title: stapelia vetula var. juvencula
intro: Stapelia vetula var. juvencula, also known as the carrion lily or the ghost flower, is a rare and unusual succulent plant belonging to the Apocynaceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used in medicine due to its purgative and emetic properties, which are primarily attributed to the presence of cardenolides and other glycosides. The medicinal properties of Stapelia vetula var. juvencula have been employed to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, thereby providing relief from these ailments. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a raw ingredient in teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity and side effects.
